game_name: Markus Ritter - Ghosts Of The Past
review_text: Just finished it, took me about 6 hours. So the game is rather short, but a well-rounded FMV gaming experience. Designed with great attention to detail. The makers call it "queer cheesy trash" - so "queer" is true, "cheesy" is true, "trash" rather less so. Of course, the story is full of clichés, but it still manages to create believable characters whose dialogues are well-recorded by the speakers. Occasional overacting included. The puzzles are mostly simple, but not simplistic. Here and there I found the solution a little "random", but perhaps that changes if you read the text snippets more carefully... All in all, the game clearly plays in the premier league of FMV adventure games alongside modern classics such as "Late Shift" and "Contradiction", although it is much more of a traditional point & click than a quick-time click orgy, fortunately there are no QTEs in the gameplay. A clear recommendation!

game_name: Markus Ritter - Ghosts Of The Past
review_text:
As a massive Gabriel Knight fan, I at first had a chuckle when I heard of this game. It's so obvious that it's a fan game. But I've also been missing those games for decades, so I knew I had to play it anyway. 

Well, turns out it's good on its own. Though not without its bugs and navigation issues, the game features  interesting characters, locations, and mechanics. It also distances itself from GK in a lot of ways, particularly with the antagonists. While in GK the 'shadows' can be any sort of supernatural beast, in Markus Ritter they are more like a species of body snatchers.

Overall, an enjoyable experience. Hopefully we see more.

game_name: Markus Ritter - Ghosts Of The Past
review_text: Wow wow wow! That was absolutely amazing. I can honestly say that this was the best game I've played in years. The drama is non-stop, there is just the right amount of challenge and no puzzle outstays its welcome - as there is a task list on the phone. The musical score is again really strong and always apt, though no big standout this time like the main song in the first installment as well as that beautiful piano piece. The places to visit and explore are interesting and a couple of the houses are amazing. It's refreshing to have a bisexual main character with a strong romantic and emotional focus on his current partner. Ludwig looks like a cute pixie / elf. There is a lot of fun in this game too - I loved reading the quirky astrology section in the daily newspaper and there are some sudden, unexpected laugh out loud moments too. There are also sprinklings of 90's point and click game references throughout, not least an article in the newspaper about holidaying on Monkey Island. In terms of tech requirements, I did get a little lag in places, most notably the club/cafe (just a short section)  but it was no game-breaker and I played it on a very old desktop computer with an i3 processor. The FMV is really crisp and high resolution. The huge effort and work that has clearly gone into the production of this game is very evident. Anyone who loves 90's style FMV games (often regarded as the golden age of point and click) will also love this game as it's up there with the best of those while being for a modern audience with modern values. Just brilliant!

game_name: Markus Ritter - Ghosts Of The Past
review_text: This game was made with a lot of love and attention to detail. Lots of hidden nods and eastereggs that point to various point & click classics. It's amazing to see a game set in my home city. Just  started out and hope to dive deeper into the adventures of a new generation of Schattenjäger! :) We will catch the killer!

game_name: Markus Ritter - Ghosts Of The Past
review_text:
I very much enjoyed both demo's and looked forward to the full game. I am not far advanced into the game as yet but, having enjoyed Gabriel Knight, I of course like the story line , characters, humour, and atmosphere.  
It is also visually appealing.

game_name: Markus Ritter - Ghosts Of The Past
review_text:
As a huge fan of FMV games, I jumped at the chance to become a Kickstarter backer for Ghosts of the Past after really enjoying The Lost Family. I wasn’t disappointed – this release has become one of my favourite results from a crowdfunding project.

While the storyline is quite serious, Ghosts of the Past has a dry sense of humour running throughout it which had us laughing at times. Adventure gamers will notice the Gabriel Knight influences and happily recognise the references to the classics. Unlike recent FMV games which seem to focus on choice and consequence, this plays more like a point-and-click. The puzzles aren’t particularly challenging but they’re enjoyable and don’t outstay their welcome.

Seeing how Markus’s character has grown between the first and second game was a treat. He starts off as someone who’s drifting along without direction, and he now has it after delving into his family’s history. I won’t say too much for fear of spoiling it, but the end of Ghosts of the Past sets him up to develop further and show a stronger side to his personality.

On that note, well done to the cast of actors! FMV games tend to be over-the-top and, although there are some appropriately campy moments here, everyone does an excellent job of making their characters believable. I wasn’t fond of Ro at the beginning, but after she drops her guard, you can see why she’s a great counterpart for Markus.

If there’s ever a Kickstarter campaign for a third Markus Ritter instalment, sign me up already.

Our playthrough can be watched here: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=3uD5pqZX-WI&list=PLUZJQ9yOnp3k8mDWZbB7MQb-apVggpn_v

game_name: Markus Ritter - Ghosts Of The Past
review_text: A fun FMV game with beautiful locations. Very interested to see where the story goes next.

game_name: Markus Ritter - Ghosts Of The Past
review_text:
Aside from this one and Flimmersoft's first title "Markus Ritter: The Lost Family", I actually have not played other games of this genre - yet. With their first game they caught my attention and with this one they got me on boat. They've sucessfully managed to foster interest for this type of games in me. Although I can't say - for my own lack of experience with these kinda games - where this one truly ranks when directly compared to other names on the playing field, I still very much enjoyed spending time with it. I'd still give them only 0/100 points possible, for they dared teasing me with a food recipe while I was hungry. :(

I'm definitely gonna check out the games that inspired the people over at Flimmersoft to create this, if only to make the wait for their next game feel shorter.
